description: Log of all significant development decisions

entries.description: Started Phase 2: Madkontrollen Absorption + Surgical Cleanup

details.approach: Systematic scanning and registration before any cleanup
details.rationale: Must understand the system before making changes. Registry-first approach prevents breaking dependencies and enables safe, informed cleanup decisions.

entries.description: Created reportEntryResolver - Central resolver for report normalization

details.problem: 20+ timestamp variants, 5+ performer variants, 15+ inline fallback chains causing data drift and maintenance crisis
details.approach: Create central resolver with stable contract, do NOT migrate runtime yet
details.chosen: Foundation-first: Create resolver, test, then migrate gradually
details.rationale: Creating resolver without runtime changes is safe. Enables testing before migration. Provides single source of truth for future use. Gradual migration reduces risk.
details.runtimeChanges: NONE - Resolver created but not yet used
risks: Future migration risk (mitigated by gradual approach)

entries.description: Implemented central audit contract for all Firestore writes

details.problem: Blandede datoer/tider og manglende 'oprettet af / udført af' på rapporter og logbog. Inconsistent audit trail across collections.
details.approach: Create central audit contract with standard fields, implement in logbooks first, plan gradual rollout
details.chosen: Foundation-first: Create auditFields.js, implement in logbooks, plan gradual migration
details.rationale: Central audit contract ensures consistency. Logbooks are safest starting point (new entries only). Gradual rollout reduces risk. Backward compatibility maintained.
implementedIn: logbooks.html (buildEntryFromForm - new entries get full audit contract)
details.runtimeChanges: Logbooks now create entries with full audit contract. Old entries still display with fallbacks.
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - resolveAuditDisplay handles legacy data gracefully with warnings
risks: Logbook entries may fail if user/profile not available
Need to ensure currentUser and currentUserProfile are set

entries.description: Implemented audit contract in rutiner.html task completion

details.problem: Task instances and entries missing consistent audit trail. Cannot track who completed tasks, when they were updated, or who changed frequency.
details.approach: Add audit contract to all task write paths in rutiner.html using central auditFields.js helpers
details.chosen: Frontend-first: Use createAuditCompleteFields and createAuditUpdateFields in all write paths
details.rationale: Frontend writes are safest to change. Audit helpers ensure consistency. Backward compatibility maintained. No schema changes needed.
filesModified: public/modules/egenkontrol/rutiner.html (added audit contract to 5 write paths)

task_instances: completedAt, completedAtClient, completedByUid, completedByName, completedByEmail (on completion)
updatedAt, updatedAtClient, updatedByUid, updatedByName, updatedByEmail (on all updates)
task_templates: updatedAt, updatedAtClient, updatedByUid, updatedByName, updatedByEmail (on frequency update)

registryUpdates: dependency-map.json: Added rutiner.html with audit dependencies
details.runtimeChanges: All task completions, overdue logging, and frequency updates now include full audit contract
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - Old entries without audit fields still work. Resolver handles gracefully.
risks: User/profile may not be available (mitigated by safe defaults)
Need to test all completion paths
mitigations: createAuditCompleteFields returns safe defaults if no user

entries.description: Fixed resolveCurrentActor to prioritize profile.profilname and avoid company names

details.phase: Phase 2C - Audit Actor Person Name Fix
details.problem: Audit trail showing company names instead of person names. New field profile.profilname created but not prioritized. Need to ensure PERSON names are used, not company names.
details.approach: Update resolveCurrentActor priority order to prioritize person name fields and actively avoid company names
details.chosen: Frontend resolver fix with company name detection and warnings
details.rationale: Frontend resolver is single source of truth for actor resolution. Adding company name detection prevents future issues. Warnings enable monitoring.
warningsAdded: actor_field_matches_company_name - when resolved name matches company name
missing_real_person_name - when no person name fields available
details.companyNameDetection: Compares resolved name against profile.companyName, profile.profile.companyName, profile.profileCompanyName, profile.name (case-insensitive)
registryUpdates: helper-registry.json: Updated resolveCurrentActor with new priority, warnings, risk level HIGH
details.runtimeChanges: All future audit trail entries will use person names. Company names actively avoided. Warnings logged when person name missing.
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - Old profilename field still supported. Fallback to email if no person name available.
risks: May show email if profilname not set
Need to ensure onboarding sets profilname

entries.description: URGENT: Fixed auditFields serverTimestamp crash and missing user in rutiner.html

details.problem: FirebaseError: Unsupported field value: custom ServerTimestampFieldValueImpl in setDoc/updateDoc. Console error: No user provided to resolveCurrentActor. Runtime crash when completing tasks.
rootCause: serverTimestamp() sentinel values not allowed in spread operations with merge
window.currentUser and window.currentUserProfile not set from setupAuthGate
resolveCurrentActor crashed when user was null
details.approach: Add useServerTimestamp option to audit helpers, store user/profile to window, safe fallbacks for missing user
details.chosen: Frontend fix with useServerTimestamp: false option and safe user fallbacks
details.rationale: Minimal change to fix crash. Client timestamps are acceptable. Firestore doesn't allow serverTimestamp in spread/merge operations. Need immediate fix.
filesModified: public/core/auditFields.js (added options parameter, safe user fallback)

auditFieldsChanges.clientFields: createdAtClient, completedAtClient, updatedAtClient always ISO string

missingUserFix.before: return { uid: null, name: 'System', email: null }

rutinerHtmlChanges.payloadLogging: console.log('[audit payload check]', { hasCompletedAt, completedAtType, completedByName, actorSource, warnings })

details.runtimeChanges: All rutiner.html audit writes now use ISO timestamps instead of serverTimestamp(). User/profile stored to window. Safe fallbacks prevent crashes.
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - Default useServerTimestamp: true for other code. Only rutiner.html uses false.
nextSteps: Consider migrating other pages to useServerTimestamp: false
risks: Client timestamps less accurate than server
Need to ensure all pages store window.currentUser

entries.description: Added onboarding contact person as audit actor source

details.problem: New onboarding stored company names for branding but did not reliably store a person name for audit fields.
details.approach: Reuse existing onboarding fields and audit resolver priority. Add contactPersonName/profilename to onboarding payloads and Functions writes without renaming collections or changing payment flow.
alternatives: Create a new user profile collection
details.chosen: Runtime-compatible field addition in existing live_user_profiles.profile and users/{uid} documents
details.rationale: resolveCurrentActor already prioritizes contactPersonName/profilename. Supplying those fields fixes new audit writes while old profiles still fall back safely.
validation: Contact person cannot equal company name
details.backwardCompatibility: Maintained. Existing companyName/displayName/name remain company branding fields and old profiles without contactPersonName still use existing audit fallbacks.
risks: Users may enter a role title instead of a real name

details.problem: No way to filter report entries by employee/person who performed the action. Needed to see which employee completed which tasks.
details.approach: Add dropdown filter that uses resolveEntryActor() to get normalized person names and filter entries client-side
details.chosen: Client-side filter using resolveEntryActor() with company name blocking
details.rationale: Client-side filtering is fast with loaded data. resolveEntryActor() already blocks company names. No Firestore schema changes needed.
filesModified: public/modules/egenkontrol/rapporter.html (added employee filter UI and logic)

uiChanges.dynamicOptions: Built from loaded entries using resolveEntryActor()

filterLogic.populateEmployeeFilter: Collects unique employee names from allEntries, excludes blocked company names and 'Ukendt medarbejder'
filterLogic.getFilteredEntries: Added employee filter check using resolveEntryActor()

companyNameBlocking.blockedCompanyName: Entries with actor.blockedCompanyName are excluded from filter options

details.filterScope: Affects all report sections: statistics, temperature logs, cleaning, receiving, deviations, all actions
details.runtimeChanges: Users can now filter reports by employee. Filter dropdown populated dynamically. Company names never shown as options.
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - No schema changes. Filter is optional (default: all).
nextSteps: Test employee filter with real data
risks: Need to ensure resolveEntryActor called correctly
mitigations: Dropdown limited to actual employees in data

entries.description: Added HACCP control point for røgning/varmrøgning (smoking/hot smoking)

details.problem: System used standard opvarmning (75°C minimum) but company performs varmrøgning of fish/salmon with documented temperatures around 70°C. This appeared as CCP deviation even when process was correct.
details.approach: Create separate canonical routine for røgning with 70°C minimum for varmrøgede fiskeprodukter, distinct from opvarmning
details.chosen: New canonical routine 'roegning' with dedicated CCP criteria
details.rationale: Røgning is a distinct process with different temperature requirements. Separating it prevents false CCP deviations and provides proper HACCP documentation.

newRoutine.criticalLimit: Minimum 70 °C i centrum for varmrøgede fiskeprodukter
newRoutine.acceptCriteria: Minimum 70 °C i centrum for varmrøgede fiskeprodukter, medmindre anden dokumenteret proces anvendes

haccpIntegration.ccpDescription: Røgning og varmrøgning med tid/temperatur kontrol

details.runtimeChanges: New roegning routine available. Auto-generated for smoke ovens. Separate from opvarmning. 70°C minimum for varmrøgede fiskeprodukter.
details.backwardCompatibility: MAINTAINED - Existing opvarmning unchanged. No migration needed. New routine only.
nextSteps: Test smoke oven in onboarding → roegning task generated
Verify rapport: shows under 'Dokumentation for røgning'
Verify NOT shown as Opvarmning or Varmholdelse
risks: Need to ensure equipment detection works
